sections.paragraph:
ἐκνήφω fut. ἐκνήψω LXX; 1 aor. ἐξένηψα ‘become sober’ (Lynceus in Athen. 4, 5 p. 130b; LXX) lit. of one recovering from a drunken revel, ‘sober up’ (Anth. Pal. 5, 135, 6); in our lit., only fig. come to one’s senses (Plut., Demosth. 20, 3; Aretaeus p. 41, 10; SibOr Fgm. 3, 41) ἐκνήψατε δικαίως sober up for uprightness (‘come to a sober and right mind’ NRSV) 1 Cor 15:34.—ELövestam, Über die ntl. Aufforderung zur Nüchternheit, StTh ’58, 83f (on gnostic connection); AMalherbe, JBL 87, ’68, 78 (on connection w. Epicurus).—M-M. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

Aretaeus Aretaeus , II a.d.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

NRSV NRSV = New Revised Standard Version of the NT—List 6

StTh StTh = Studia Theologica (Lund)—List 6

JBL JBL = Journal of Biblical Literature—List 6

Epicurus Epicurus , IV–III b.c.—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 307
